name: Milkfish
description: Farmed in large numbers across the region, especially in Davao del Sur and Davao del Norte.
location: Davao del Sur, Davao del Norte

name: Sea Cow
description: A large, herbivorous marine mammal found in seagrass meadows along Davao del Norte and Davao Oriental.

name: Short-bodied Mackerel
description: A popular, fast-swimming species often found in local markets, it is commonly caught in shallow coastal waters.

name: Sting Ray
description: These large rays are found in the open waters of Davao Gulf, often observed by divers in protected marine areas.

name: Green Sea Turtle
description: Nesting areas for this endangered species are found in the islands of Davao Occidental.

name: Goby
description: Commonly found in tide pools and shallow reefs in coastal Davao.

name: Tabios
description: The world’s smallest commercially harvested fish, often found in protected areas around Davao.

name: Sardine
description: Small, oily fish abundant in coastal waters, used in many regional dishes.

name: Freshwater Sardine
description: Endemic to Taal Lake, but also found in specific, isolated freshwater regions of Davao's lakes.
